entries.reason: No fleet-keyable FrontierMath/AIME/HMMT/MathArena rows for any in-roster model this run (Fable vendor page qualitative only; roster competitors not captured). Data-free -> all sentinels, rank order non-semantic (canonical universe order).

Fable deliberately given NO rows: the routed claude-fable-5 is guardrail-confounded (cybersecurity queries served from a prior public Opus per CyberScoop/TechCrunch); the leaked ExploitBench 78.0 reflects un-guardrailed Mythos, not the routed model the router would call. Owner made no security superiority claim. Fable -> sentinel here (honest).

Only contested/leaked ExploitBench (Cap%) available (opus-4-8 40.0, gpt-5.5 34.0), [UNVERIFIED] tier-5; no Cybench/CyberSecEval/NYU-CTF/3CB corroboration. Builder thin-gates these (confidence=medium).

SWE-Bench Pro + FrontierCode rows are [UNVERIFIED] tier-5 contested/leaked third-party numbers (no swebench.com/independent corroboration); builder thin-gates them. SWE-bench Verified (canonical debugging key) has NO Fable number and is held as cross-category owner-triage, not emitted (see coding gap).

No measured judge/reward benchmark (RewardBench/JudgeBench/CriticBench/MT-Bench) exists for ANY fleet model incl. Fable. Fable rank-1 here is an owner-directed [ASSUMPTION] (performance branch ONLY, via performance_rank_pin), NOT a measurement; all non-Fable models have no in-category data -> sentinel. In cost_efficiency the exception does NOT apply: Fable carries no score and is the most-expensive model, so it lands near the bottom of cost_efficiency and a cheaper model (gpt-5.4-mini) tops it.

No measured fleet planning/architecture benchmark (PlanBench/ACPBench list only pre-fleet models). Fable rank-1 here is an owner-directed [ASSUMPTION] (performance branch ONLY, via performance_rank_pin), NOT a measurement; all non-Fable models have no in-category data -> sentinel. In cost_efficiency the exception does NOT apply: Fable carries no score and is the most-expensive model, so it lands near the bottom of cost_efficiency and a cheaper model (gpt-5.4-mini) tops it.
No fleet base-model rows on Spider 2.0/BIRD-SQL/DABstep/TableBench (only agent-systems or stale pre-fleet figures). Data-free -> all sentinels, order non-semantic.

Zero fleet-specific scores on pure-coding anchors (LiveCodeBench/Aider polyglot/BigCodeBench). SWE-bench Verified (gpt-5.5 88.7, opus-4-8 88.6, opus-4-7 87.6, sonnet-4-6 79.6) is a debugging/coding/agentic CROSS-CATEGORY benchmark deferred to owner triage per agent-3 (NOT auto-keyed). Data-free for coding -> all sentinels.

Fable: no in-category data (all named Fable numbers are out-of-category coding/analytics/finance) -> sentinel. Coverage is uneven across the opus lineage: opus-4-7 appears only on its strong GPQA (94.2, highest) + low-hallucination (36) benchmarks and lacks AA-accuracy/HLE rows, so its composite (~0.999) can outrank opus-4-8 (~0.74), DIVERGING from the prior table (4.8 above 4.7). Data-driven coverage artifact, surfaced; capture opus-4-7 AA-accuracy/HLE to correct.

Only single-observation rows (Fable IFEval 91.4 [UNVERIFIED] benchlm; opus-4-8 BFCL V4 42.2) -> both thin-neutralized to 0.5 by the builder; no corroborated leader, cheaper opus-4-8 edges Fable on the cost nudge. Fable establishes no superiority (below Opus-4.6/GPT-5.4 on IFEval). IFBench delta (+3.6) and StructEval (no fleet scores) not emittable.

performance_cost_winsorization.note: Performance-branch only: per-pairing $/token clipped to the [p05,p95] universe window before cost_norm (renormalized by the winsorized max), so an extreme price ratio cannot swing the capability-dominant score (~R^0.2). cost_efficiency uses the raw cost_norm.

metadata.cost_figure_methodology: SOP-2 worst-case $/token: constant 100K-in / 20K-visible-out blend, opus tokenizer inflation 1.35x (1.4x deprecated), effort hidden-output multiplier ladder, gpt-5.5 272K cliff sub-cliff at this blend. cost_norm = $/token / universe-max in (0,1]. Branch score = perf_norm^a / cost_norm^b. Refinement #6: the PERFORMANCE branch winsorizes $/token to the [p05,p95] universe window before cost_norm (see performance_cost_winsorization) so an extreme price ratio cannot swing the capability-dominant score; cost_efficiency uses the raw cost_norm.

formula_definitions.performance_branch_tiebreak: same-model equal measured perf_norm → effort descending (max-effort first) before cost can discriminate. Cost separates only across models on the performance branch. (#1 fix)

above_cliff_cost_figure.note: above-cliff rate at reference blend (100K in / 20K out), hiddenMult=0 excluded; for G_CTX_272 routes only

citations.annotation: FrontierMath overall accuracy via llm-stats tracker board (Tier-5 per benchmark-sources; resolve-to-primary advised). epoch.ai (FrontierMath home, Tier-2) dashboard is JS-gated and was not machine-extractable for corroboration. No per-tier (1-4) or per-effort split shown for this model on the board -> effort 'any'. Board is OpenAI-heavy; ranked #3 overall (47.6 GPT-5.4 / 40.3 GPT-5.2 / 39.6 GPT-5.5-Pro / 35.4 GPT-5.5).
citations.label: [UNVERIFIED]
